linkedin description: Our mission is to help exhausted parents and restless children around the world with a unique sleep solution. Since introducing our beds to so many families, the feedback has been overwhelming with relief, hope, and excitement. With each delivery of a zPod, we feel like pages are turning and new chapters are beginning. As a parent, imagine a time where your child was struggling. It was probably challenging to see your child struggle… especially when you feel powerless and do not have the answers. We understand how that feels. We’ve seen children sleeping in their closets, under their beds, or barely at all. And when your child doesn’t sleep well, you don’t sleep well. As a new chapter begins when trying one of our beds, families are finding a belief that something different can happen. And as this belief continues to grow, it gives families all over the world a belief that they can begin a new chapter as well. The chapter of better rest, less exhaustion, and more love in the household. summary: Most service businesses don’t struggle because they aren’t trying hard enough. They’ve reached a point where sales and marketing can’t keep running on different playbooks. Results feel inconsistent. Deals take too long. Sales isn't talking to enough right fit prospects. Marketing activity increases—but revenue doesn’t follow. That’s usually when leaders start asking better questions: → What’s actually driving growth here? → What needs to change—and what’s just noise? The answer isn’t more effort. It’s better strategic alignment. That’s where I come in. I’ve spent over a decade advising leadership teams across financial services, professional services, and B2B organizations—helping them make sense of complexity and focus on what actually drives growth. What I Help Businesses Navigate I work primarily with B2B and professional service firms at key growth inflection points, including: - Scaling beyond founder-led sales - Clarifying positioning as the business matures - Moving from opportunistic growth to intentional growth - Preparing for acquisition, merger, or exit A core specialty of my work is helping businesses realign sales and marketing when misalignment is costing momentum, margin, or morale. How I Work - Strategic assessments and marketing roadmaps - On-site and virtual workshops for leadership teams - Fractional strategic advisory (fCMO) partnerships Many of our clients operate on EOS or work with a Pinnacle guide or business coach. They’ve done the foundational work—but now need a clear, practical sales and marketing strategy that aligns everyone around a simple, effective plan for growth. This isn’t about adding another framework. It’s about translating leadership vision into focused execution teams can rally around. The Results Clients Experience - Clear positioning and messaging sales can confidently lead with - Buyers who are already aligned before the first sales conversation - Faster, higher-value sales conversations with fewer wrong-fit prospects - Less wasted sales effort and shorter sales cycles - Reduced friction between teams - Greater confidence in growth decisions We intentionally work with a small number of right-fit clients each year. When we’re not the best fit, we refer businesses to trusted strategic advisors from our vetted network. Our process and pricing are transparent and clearly outlined on our website. I also train experienced marketers to step into high-value strategic advisory roles, helping them move beyond execution and lead at a level that truly impacts results.

summary: Turning a lifelong love for waterways, marketing, and technology into a company! Proud co-founder of Ballyhoo Media, a family-owned company innovating the maritime and out-of-home advertising industry by uniquely connecting brands with people on America's waterways. We started this company with little more than a love for the water and an audacious idea. Zero knowledge of outdoor advertising or boats? No problem. It turns out that sometimes, naivety plus a giant screen on a boat can be a recipe for success. Since setting sail in 2016, Ballyhoo Media has become the world's leading water-based media company. Our fleet of American-made digital billboard boats has empowered businesses, from Fortune 500s to local storefronts, to make waves with unique advertising and experiential campaigns. Beyond our boats, we've teamed up with hundreds of vessels nationwide, creating unforgettable activations and bringing unique forms of advertising to waterfront communities like never before. But our platforms aren't the only thing that makes us unique. Ballyhoo Media was born from our love for the waterways, and our mission stretches beyond advertising. We're committed to enhancing our waters, organizing unforgettable events, supporting local businesses, and joining hands with communities and local governments. We stand firm in preserving and protecting our oceans, adopting "blue technologies" in our boats, and spreading non-profit messaging, all to ensure our waterways are around for future generations to enjoy. Now, we're harnessing our unique expertise in waterway advertising and technologies to spearhead further innovation within the passenger vessel industry. Our goal? To inject new life into the industry and enrich the passenger experience on ferries, water taxis, and tour boats across America. At Ballyhoo Media, we envision the water as an untapped canvas, a space for meaningful communication, and we're excited about the waves we're creating in this arena. When I'm not rocking a bathing suit and making a splash with Ballyhoo Media, you catch me soaking up live music with my best friend, Don Julio, watching my Baltimore Ravens on the road to a super bowl, cruising top-down in my Ford Bronco, and always saying 'yes' to weekend plans.

summary: I’ve spent the last 20+ years helping agencies win business and learning firsthand what actually works. I started my first company in high school, building a concert promotions business that grew into a multimillion-dollar experiential marketing firm. Along the way, we worked with Grammy Award–winning artists like Lady Gaga and Pitbull and activated brand programs for Disney, Toyota, and Anheuser-Busch. Then the 2008 recession hit. Like many agencies, we had grown on referrals and reputation, and when demand stopped, so did the pipeline. That experience pushed me deep into business development. I sold my agency, rebuilt my skill set, and spent the next decade leading new business and growth programs at independent and holding company agencies. I’ve helped agencies generate tens of millions of dollars in opportunities and win work with brands like Kohl’s, Constellation Brands, Netflix, Heineken, and Autodesk. During the pandemic, history repeated itself—agencies losing clients, pipelines drying up. This time, I knew how to help. I left a senior agency role and founded NextBigWin to help agencies see opportunity earlier, earn trust sooner, and stop competing only at the RFP stage. Today, in addition to leading NextBigWin, I host "CMO Journeys," a video interview series featuring senior marketing leaders who discuss their perspectives on growth, partnerships, and agency relationships. I am a member of the 4A’s Expert Network, where I advise agencies on modern, signal-driven growth strategies. If you run or lead growth at an agency selling high-consideration services and want better timing, better access, and fewer cold starts, I’m always open to a conversation.

Most agencies don’t struggle because of talent. They struggle because meaningful access happens too late. By the time an RFP is issued or a shortlist forms, relationships already exist and influence is limited. summary: Most organizations think they have a brand. What they actually have is a logo, a mission statement, and a marketing budget — which makes them a commodity with good packaging. There's no shame in that. It's where most organizations live. But it's expensive to stay there — because commodities compete on price, volume, and hope. Brands compete on meaning. And meaning wins. I'm here to serve mission-driven leaders who suspect there's a gear they haven't found yet. The ones building something that matters — and who sense that the distance between where they are and where they should be isn't simply a marketing problem. Spoiler alert: It's a strategy problem. As your embedded Fractional Chief Strategy Officer, I don't just parachute in with a deck and disappear. We will (together) identify your strategy, innovate and integrate it across every function of your organization — operations, culture, delivery, engagement — and provide ongoing leadership to protect and scale it. Not as a communications exercise. As an organizational discipline. The result? Differentiation that sticks. Loyalty that compounds. Valuation that reflects what you've actually built. We've done this for 15+ organizations across healthcare, financial services, wine, nonprofits, startups, and beyond — building, tightening, defending, and scaling strategies that turn purpose into market position. If you're tired of being the best-kept secret in your category — or worse, being seen as interchangeable with your competitors — that's the gap we close. Send me a DM and let's book a discovery call. We'll find out together whether you're a brand or a commodity. And if you're not sure, that's exactly where we start.
job title: CEO, Brand Builder, Fractional CSO & Vision Coach
job description: Here's an uncomfortable question: Are you a brand, or a commodity in a brand wrapper? Most leaders answer quickly. The honest answer takes longer — because real brand isn't what you say about yourself. It's whether your strategy makes you the only choice for the people who matter most to your mission. Corridor Strategy Group is a fractional Chief Strategy Officer practice for mission-driven organizations that are done treating strategy like a subset of marketing. Because that's how brands become commodities — slowly, then all at once. What started as an award-winning brand strategy and creative firm serving disruptors evolved into something more fundamental: embedded strategy leadership. summary: I built Customer Success as a growth mechanism. The goal was always revenue — specifically, getting more of it from customers companies had already acquired. Not to make customers happy. Not to reduce churn as an end in itself. To get customers to outcomes that matter to their business, because that's the only model where the commercial results actually follow. I formalized the discipline. Co-authored the book (Wiley, 2016). Watched the field spend a decade trying to pretend CS wasn't commercial. Took the message to sales leaders and CROs instead, framed it in revenue terms, and found the same universal problem everywhere: companies with customer bases full of untapped lifetime value and no system to capture it. That's what I've been building since. LTV:Max is the framework and resources for maximizing customer lifetime value systematically. Extensible Agents is what CS becomes when agentic AI removes the bottleneck that was always the customer — and frees your team to do the work only humans can do. The same inflection point that happened in 2012, when Customer Success went from instinct to discipline, is happening again. The teams that formalize agentic CS first will define what this function looks like for the next decade. If you run CS at a SaaS company and you're trying to figure out what this means for your team: sixteenventures.com, ltvmax.com, extensibleagents.com.
job description: For 15 years, Customer Success organizations were told what they could and could not do. By the systems they were given access to. By the gatekeepers who controlled those systems. By their own beliefs about what was possible. That sentence — "I wish we could just do that for the customer, but we just can't" — just became obsolete. Extensible Agents is built for CS teams who are ready to stop conforming to systems that were never built for them. We build agentic workflows that absorb the complexity customers were never going to handle themselves, free CS teams to do the work only humans can do, and ask not what is the least we can do for a customer — but what is the most. The same inflection point that happened in 2012, when Customer Success went from an instinct to a discipline, is happening again. The teams that formalize agentic CS first will define what this function looks like for the next decade. We built this company for those teams.
headline: I invented Customer Success. Now I’m building what comes after it.

linkedin description: L’Ottawa International Cup est un tournoi de football 7v7 unique en son genre, organisé pour réunir des joueurs et des supporters de cultures et d'horizons divers autour de leur passion commune pour le sport. Développement de l'événement : Avec le succès de sa première édition, l’Ottawa International Cup continue d'élargir sa portée en impliquant plus d'écoles, d'associations étudiantes, et de partenaires locaux.
